players.name: The Ages
players.title: Founder of the Federation
players.bio: He that is called The Ages did ordain the fellowship known as The Chess Federation, the which served not as a common guild, but as a privy covenant of degree amongst the brethren. By his hand was the covenant framed: if The Ages vanquish a player, that player is straightway ordained into the Chess Federation; if a player vanquish The Ages, that player is judged unmeet and cast out into exile, until such time as a future defeat shall make the scales even. Thus, ruination served as the seal of fellowship, whilst triumph against the founder wrought a season of banishment.

players.name: Holzem, the Elder
players.title: The Sacrificial
players.bio: The son of Pa Beth, and Ma Beth, called 'the Elder,' who seemeth to have been of three and forty years in the days of these recorded battles. He loved a fierce assault, betimes casting away his pieces. One hoarded missive declareth that he entreated his men as Abraham entreated his sons. Scholars judge this saying was spoken in jest, yet it showeth a ready heart to sacrifice his host to weave cunning snares. For this cause, the later glosses of the recovered scrolls name him: 'The Sacrificial.' He is also remembered for the Oration of the Salmon, given within an alehouse by the Green Bay, wherein he declared that men must let the salmon run.

players.name: Holzem the Youthful
players.title: The Exiled Victor
players.bio: The son of Pa Beth, and Ma Beth, called 'the Youthful,' being of forty years. He is known as The Exiled Victor — for by the inward laws of the Federation's covenant, whatsoever man shall vanquish The Ages is cast out for a season, until the founder may cast him down in a battle yet to come. He had overthrown the father of the order, yet by the framing of the covenant, that very conquest made him unmeet for the fellowship. Therefore did triumph bring forth the utter opposite of preferment. It brought forth exile. And there lieth a wandering proverb, with no master to own it, stating that he who striketh the bell is cast down from the tower.

Rb6 hxg2 49. Bxg2 Kxg2 50. Rxb3 Qf1 51. Rb2+ Kg3 52. Rb5 Qxb5 53. Kd2 e4
games.lore_description: One of the most exact treatises of the chronicle telleth of a fray betwixt The Ages and Holzem, the Elder. The contest came to a wearying siege about a single pawn that did bind both kingdoms. Lo, a pawn stood betwixt the realms, and neither host might march forward lest ruin fall upon it. In the fullness of time, the matter was judged in favor of The Ages. Holzem, the Elder yielded up his sword. Within the laws of the Federation's covenant, this end did make fast his place within the Federation, for his ordination came by the hand of the founder that struck him down.

games.lore_description: Ere the battle began, shreds of parley do show Holzem the Youthful speaking of late sorrows, of gold lost at the gaming tables and vexations of the purse. Yet whilst the battle raged, The Ages gathered unto himself a great host of advantage, the which he did boast of. Notwithstanding this manifest favor, the latter end of the battle turned in strange wise. Holzem the Youthful made fast his footing and by slow degrees did turn the struggle unto a triumphant end. By the inward laws of the Federation's covenant, this victory wrought exile — for he who striketh the bell is cast down from the tower.
